type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
1) A revised emission factor was used to calculate PM10 emissions from combustion sources.  
2) The PM10 emissions from process sources were updated. 
3) Removal of PM10 emissions due to road dust since facility had less than 10,000 VKT on unpaved roads (reporting requirement).
In this update, the revised combustion emission factor was used; PM10 emissions from process sources was calculated and reported based on total particulate matter source testing results and using US EPA PM ratios; PM10 emissions from road dust was not included since they did not meet the reporting requirement (10,000 vehicle km travelled on unpaved roads).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
1) The report was updated in include PM2.5 emissions from combustion sources. 
2) The report was updated in include PM2.5 emissions from process sources. 
In this update, PM2.5 emissions from combustion sources were calculated based on U.S EPA emission factors, and PM2.5 emissions from process sources were based on total particulate matter source testing results and calculated using US EPA PM2.5 ratios.

type.en: Reason why substance was added to the report
type.fr: Raison pour laquelle la substance a été ajoutée au rapport

comments.comment: Calculation methodology reviewed during preparation of Toxic Substance Reduction Plan in 2013.  This required a re-calculation of previous years emissions and inclusion of PM2.5.  PM2.5 emission rates from each production source were estimated using the PM emission rate and a PM/PM2.5 ratio, as advised by Environment Canada in December 2013.  Ratios were obtained from the US EPA, AP-42 using the Source Classification Code (SCC) for the process equipment.  Similarly, SCCs were applied to calculate PM2.5 emissions from combustion equipment (i.e. boiler).  In addition, PM2.5 emissions were also included for consistency.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The chloroform is in water releaes to ground water recharge. The compounds are in the ffluent water. We do not discharge to a surface water body. We use percolation beds in the winter, and irrigation in the summer.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: In 2000, we sent some elemental mercury out for recycling. The mercury was from mercury float manometers that had been removed from service. This year, we are reporting the amounts from discarded fluorescent tubes for the first time.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: We recycled 25 kgs. of mercury from mercury float manometers that had been removed form service in 2000. We are planning to send fluorescent tubes to a recycler, and we are also investigating low mercury content fluorescent tubes.
